The Technical Edge

GridScore

Rank points of connection and assess grid risk before committing development capital.

REQUEST SAMPLE REPORT

Analysis Framework

What GridScore does

  • Automated technical assessment of substation capacity and network constraints across priority markets.

  • Connection success modelling based on approval patterns, historical queues, and regional load profiles.

  • Real-time risk scoring for curtailment exposure, congestion pressure, and deployment timing.

98%

Data confidence

400+

Substations tracked

15ms

Query speed

Tier 1

Bankable data

Strategic Context

Why grid data matters now

As the energy transition accelerates, the grid has become the ultimate bottleneck. Project failure rates rise when interconnection risk is discovered after land, capital, and engineering work are already committed.

Securing development capital requires empirical proof of connection feasibility. GridScore provides the technical validation needed by developers, lenders, and investment committees.

  • Mitigate interconnection risk
  • Optimize asset deployment

Operational Flow

How GridScore is delivered

1

Location Intake

Provide GPS coordinates, substation IDs, or a proposed development pipeline for analysis.

2

Analytical Pass

GridScore cross-references connection constraints, network capacity signals, and historical queue data.

3

Risk Dossier

Receive a concise technical report and ranked view of which grid positions deserve diligence.

Beyond the Score

Grid Strategy Support

Data is only as valuable as the decisions it informs. Revolt pairs GridScore with technical advisory support to help developers navigate filings, capacity constraints, and BESS sizing decisions.

Interconnection Filing Support

Expert guidance on DNO, TSO, and utility application optimization.

Curtailment Analysis

Downside-risk protection through constraint, congestion, and nodal-signal review.

Ready to find your strongest grid position?

Use Gridscore to compare connection risk, capacity signals, and project viability before capital is committed.
